flashcards.back: A regression technique that models the relationship between a dependent variable and two or more independent variables: Y = b0 + b1X1 + b2X2 + ... + bnXn + error.

flashcards.front: How does multiple regression differ from simple regression?
flashcards.back: Simple regression uses one predictor variable (Y = b0 + b1X). Multiple regression uses two or more predictor variables, allowing it to capture the combined effect of multiple factors.

When two or more independent variables are highly correlated with each other. It makes it difficult to determine the individual effect of each predictor and can lead to unstable coefficient estimates.
Using Variance Inflation Factor (VIF). VIF > 5 suggests moderate multicollinearity, VIF > 10 indicates serious multicollinearity. Correlation matrices also help identify correlated features.

A modified version of R-squared that penalizes adding unnecessary predictors. Unlike R-squared which always increases with more features, adjusted R-squared decreases if added features do not improve the model.

flashcards.front: What does each coefficient bi represent?
flashcards.back: The expected change in Y for a one-unit increase in Xi, holding all other variables constant (ceteris paribus). This is the partial effect of each predictor.

flashcards.front: What is the F-test in multiple regression?
flashcards.back: A statistical test that determines whether the overall regression model is significant - whether at least one predictor has a non-zero coefficient. It compares the model to a mean-only model.

flashcards.front: What is the matrix form of multiple regression?
flashcards.back: Y = Xb + e, where Y is the response vector, X is the design matrix, b is the coefficient vector, and e is the error vector. Solution: b = (X^T X)^(-1) X^T Y.

flashcards.front: Why might adding more features not improve a multiple regression model?
flashcards.back: Adding irrelevant features increases model complexity without improving fit, can introduce multicollinearity, and reduces adjusted R-squared. This is why feature selection matters.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between R-squared and Adjusted R-squared?
flashcards.back: R-squared always increases with more features (even irrelevant ones). Adjusted R-squared accounts for the number of predictors and only increases if the new feature improves the model significantly.
